payload.source_text:
QUALITY POLICY STATEMENT
Novus Mechanical Services Limited has established a Quality Policy to be consistent with the purpose and context of our organisation.

It provides a framework for the setting and review of objectives in addition to our commitment to satisfy applicable customers’, regulatory and legislative requirements as well as our commitment to continually improve our management system.
Customer focus: As an organisation we have made a commitment to understand our current and future customers’ needs; meet their requirements and strive to exceed their expectations.
Leadership: Our Top Management have committed to creating and maintaining a working environment in which people become fully involved in achieving our objectives.
Engagement of people: As an organisation we recognise that people are the essence of any good business and that their full involvement enables their abilities to be used for our benefit.

Process approach: As an organisation we understand that a desired result is achieved more efficiently when activities and related resources are managed as a process or series of interconnected processes.
Improvement: We have committed to achieving continual improvement across all aspects of our quality management system; it is one of our main annual objectives.
Evidence-based decision making: As an organisation we have committed to only make decisions relating to our QMS following an analysis of relevant data and information.

Relationship management: We recognises that an organisation and the relationship it has with its external providers are interdependent and a mutually beneficial relationship enhances the ability of both to create value.
Our policy is also to meet the requirements of our social, environmental, charitable and legislative responsibilities.

Signed : 							Date: 22/4/2025
           	
Damian Shacklock
Director
NOVUS MECHANICAL SERVICES LTD.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Source elements present in the ledger.

payload.evaluated_scope: All input material in this project

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Quality management governance across the enterprise using evidence-based decision making
payload.justification: SG278, SG279, and SG285 all describe quality management as a strategic governance function spanning policy establishment and decision-making. | SG285 establishes evidence-based decision making as a strategic governance function within quality management.

payload.description: Objective-setting process within quality framework
payload.justification: SG279 identifies objective-setting as a defined process purpose within the quality policy framework.

payload.description: Process approach methodology for achieving organizational results
payload.justification: SG283 explicitly declares the process approach as a strategic methodology for achieving desired results.

payload.description: Quality policy framework aligned with organizational context
payload.justification: SG278 and SG279 both describe the quality policy as a distinct strategic governance entity that establishes foundational frameworks and purpose for objective-setting.

payload.description: Quality policy alignment to business purpose and organizational context
payload.justification: SG278 explicitly describes the relationship between quality policy and business purpose/context as an alignment governance pattern.

payload.description: Customer needs and expectations as organizational information assets
payload.justification: SG280 identifies customer needs and expectations as strategic things that must be understood and measured, representing key information entities.

payload.description: Work environment characteristics enabling objective achievement
payload.justification: SG281 describes the work environment as a strategic entity with attributes that enable organizational objectives.

payload.description: External provider relationship structure for value creation
payload.justification: SG286 explicitly describes mutually beneficial relationships with external providers as strategic structural elements.

payload.description: Annual quality improvement cycle as organizational planning period
payload.justification: SG284 describes annual continual improvement objectives, indicating a strategic annual planning and review cycle.

payload.description: Customers whose needs and expectations drive organizational strategy
payload.justification: SG280 identifies customers as key strategic stakeholders whose needs and expectations are organizational drivers.

payload.description: Leadership role in creating enabling work environment
payload.justification: SG281 describes leadership as a distinct role with responsibility for creating enabling conditions.

payload.description: People engaged in organizational value creation
payload.justification: SG282 recognizes people as strategic actors whose engagement enables business benefit.

payload.description: External providers in mutually beneficial relationships
payload.justification: SG286 identifies external providers as strategic stakeholders in value creation relationships.

payload.description: Organizational director role with policy authorization authority
payload.justification: SG288 explicitly identifies the director role as having formal governance authority and accountability for policy.

payload.description: Understanding and exceeding customer expectations
payload.justification: SG280 explicitly articulates this as a strategic organizational driver and commitment.

payload.description: Achieving organizational objectives through enabling environment
payload.justification: SG281 identifies organizational objective achievement as the goal motivating work environment creation.

payload.description: Continual improvement across quality management
payload.justification: SG284 establishes continual improvement as a core strategic objective driving organizational activity.

payload.description: Creating value through external provider collaboration
payload.justification: SG286 identifies value creation as the strategic driver for external provider relationships.

payload.description: People engagement as essential enabler for business benefit
payload.justification: SG282 declares people engagement as an essential organizational value and strategic principle.

payload.description: Process approach for organizational efficiency
payload.justification: SG283 declares the process approach as an organizational efficiency principle guiding strategic design.

payload.description: Evidence-based decision making for quality governance
payload.justification: SG285 establishes evidence-based decision making as a fundamental principle governing organizational choices.

payload.description: Regulatory compliance requirements on quality management
payload.justification: SG279 identifies regulatory compliance as a strategic constraint shaping quality policy framework.

payload.description: Social responsibility obligations on organizational activity
payload.justification: SG287 declares social responsibilities as organizational obligations constraining strategic choices. | SG287 declares environmental responsibilities as organizational obligations constraining strategic choices. | SG287 declares charitable responsibilities as organizational obligations constraining strategic choices.

payload.description: Legislative compliance obligations on organizational activity
payload.justification: SG287 declares legislative responsibilities as organizational obligations constraining strategic choices.

payload.description: Customer focus process encompassing needs understanding, requirements meeting, and expectations exceeding
payload.justification: SG449 articulates customer focus as a business concept with distinct process activities: understanding needs, meeting requirements, and exceeding expectations.

payload.description: Leadership process for creating enabling environments and achieving objectives through people
payload.justification: SG450 defines leadership as a conceptual responsibility involving process activities of environment creation and people enablement.

payload.description: Process approach management where activities and resources are managed as interconnected processes
payload.justification: SG452 explicitly establishes process approach as a conceptual model of managing interconnected processes, representing a meta-process.

payload.description: Continual improvement process applied across quality management system
payload.justification: SG453 defines continual improvement as a central business objective requiring process execution across all QMS aspects.

payload.description: Evidence-based decision making process requiring data analysis
payload.justification: SG454 establishes evidence-based decision making as a business rule requiring a process of data analysis before decisions.

payload.description: Relationship management process for external provider interdependencies
payload.justification: SG455 defines relationship management as a conceptual model requiring process activities to manage interdependent external relationships.

payload.description: Objective-setting function within policy framework
payload.justification: SG448 identifies objective-setting as a distinct function component of the policy framework.

payload.description: Data analysis must precede quality management system decisions
payload.justification: SG454 explicitly establishes evidence-based decision making as a business rule requiring data analysis before decisions.

payload.description: Activities and resources must be managed as interconnected processes
payload.justification: SG452 establishes process approach as a normative business rule requiring interconnected process management.

payload.description: Quality policy as organizational policy framework document
payload.justification: SG447 and SG448 establish quality policy as a distinct business entity with defined scope and attributes including objectives, requirements, compliance, and improvement commitments.

payload.description: Customer requirement as business data object
payload.justification: SG448 and SG449 identify customer requirements as a distinct data object within the policy framework and customer focus concept.

payload.description: Regulatory compliance obligation as business data object
payload.justification: SG448 explicitly identifies regulatory compliance as a component of the policy framework, representing a trackable business entity.

payload.description: External provider relationship as business entity
payload.justification: SG455 defines external provider relationships as distinct interdependent entities within the business model.

payload.description: Quality policy alignment to organizational purpose and context
payload.justification: SG447 establishes alignment as a conceptual relationship between quality policy and organizational purpose/context.

payload.description: External provider value co-creation relationship
payload.justification: SG455 explicitly defines mutual value creation as the relationship model between organization and external providers.

payload.description: Continual improvement cycle applied across quality management system
payload.justification: SG453 defines continual improvement as a central objective, implying an ongoing cycle rather than one-time event.

payload.description: Quality management system decision point requiring evidence analysis
payload.justification: SG454 establishes that quality management system decisions trigger the requirement for evidence-based analysis.

payload.description: Customer as business actor with needs, requirements, and expectations
payload.justification: SG448 and SG449 identify customers as distinct business actors with specific attributes (needs, requirements, expectations) within the policy framework.

payload.description: People as organizational capability assets requiring engagement
payload.justification: SG451 identifies people as a core business concept and essential organizational assets, representing a distinct actor category.

payload.description: External provider as business actor in interdependent relationship
payload.justification: SG455 explicitly identifies external providers as actors in interdependent relationships that create mutual value.

payload.description: Leadership role responsible for creating enabling environments and achieving objectives
payload.justification: SG450 defines leadership as a conceptual responsibility, representing a distinct business role with specific accountabilities.

payload.description: Regulatory authority as organizational stakeholder
payload.justification: SG448 and SG456 reference regulatory compliance and legislative responsibility, implying regulatory authorities as external organizational stakeholders.

payload.description: Achieve organizational purpose through quality policy alignment
payload.justification: SG447 establishes alignment with organizational purpose as the foundational goal of the quality policy.

payload.description: Meet and exceed customer expectations through quality management system
payload.justification: SG448 and SG449 identify meeting customer requirements as a core goal of the policy framework and customer focus concept. | SG449 explicitly identifies exceeding expectations as a business goal within the customer focus concept.

payload.description: Enable objective achievement through people involvement
payload.justification: SG450 defines objective achievement through people involvement as the goal of leadership responsibility.

payload.description: Achieve efficiency through interconnected process management
payload.justification: SG452 explicitly identifies efficiency as the goal of the process approach conceptual model.

payload.description: Drive continual improvement across all quality management system aspects
payload.justification: SG453 explicitly defines continual improvement as a central business objective, representing a core organizational goal.

payload.description: Create mutual value through external provider relationships
payload.justification: SG455 explicitly identifies mutual value creation as the goal of relationship management with external providers.

payload.description: Customer focus as fundamental business principle
payload.justification: SG449 articulates customer focus as a core business concept, representing a foundational principle guiding organizational behavior.

payload.description: Leadership responsibility as fundamental business principle
payload.justification: SG450 defines leadership as a conceptual responsibility, representing a fundamental principle of organizational management.

payload.description: People engagement as fundamental business principle recognizing human capabilities as essential assets
payload.justification: SG451 explicitly identifies people engagement as a core business concept and principle recognizing human capabilities as essential.

payload.description: Process approach as fundamental business principle for activity management
payload.justification: SG452 establishes process approach as a conceptual model, representing a fundamental principle for how activities should be managed.

payload.description: Evidence-based decision making as fundamental business principle
payload.justification: SG454 explicitly establishes evidence-based decision making as a business rule, representing a fundamental principle for organizational decisions.

payload.description: Interdependent relationship management as fundamental business principle
payload.justification: SG455 defines relationship management as a conceptual model of interdependence, representing a fundamental principle for external engagement.

payload.description: Regulatory compliance and legislative responsibility requirements constrain and must be incorporated in policy framework
payload.justification: SG448 identifies regulatory compliance as a component of the policy framework, representing a constraining requirement on policy scope. | SG456 extends policy scope to encompass legislative responsibility, representing a constraining requirement on organizational compliance. | SG456 extends policy scope to encompass social responsibility, representing a constraining requirement on organizational behavior. | SG456 extends policy scope to encompass environmental responsibility, representing a constraining requirement on organizational behavior. | SG456 extends policy scope to encompass charitable responsibility, representing a constraining requirement on organizational behavior.

payload.description: Analyze data and information to support decision making
payload.justification: SG464 implies a logical function of analysis that must occur within the QMS. The evidence-based principle necessitates a data and information analysis function at the conceptual level.

payload.description: Process approach as logical management philosophy for achieving results through interconnected activities
payload.justification: SG463 explicitly articulates a principle describing a logical management philosophy. The process approach is a design principle establishing how activities and resources should be organized conceptually.

payload.description: Evidence-based decision making requiring analysis of data and information
payload.justification: SG464 describes a principle establishing a logical requirement for how QMS decisions must be made. This is a design principle governing decision-making approach at the conceptual level.

payload.description: Temporal validity date attribute of policy document
payload.justification: Signal references 'temporal validity' as a characteristic of the policy document. At Row 5, this represents a detailed data attribute defining the valid date range or effective period of the document entity.

payload.description: Director signature attribute identifying authorizing individual
payload.justification: Signature block is a data element that captures the identity and authorization mark of the Director. At the implementer level, this is a specific attribute field of the policy document entity.

payload.description: Director role with signature authority for policy document approval
payload.justification: Signal describes a Director signature block identifying organizational authority. At Row 5 (Implementer), this represents a specific role interface with document signing capability. Mapped to Role rather than Actor as it describes the organizational function/authority level rather than a specific individual.

payload.description: Policy document requires Director-level signature authorization for validity
payload.justification: The presence of a Director signature block as an identifying element of organizational authority implies a validation rule: the document is not valid without this specific level of approval. This is a detailed constraint on document authorization at Row 5.

payload.description: Establishes organizational quality policy aligned with business purpose and context as foundational governance framework.
Defines quality policy framework purpose for objective-setting and commitment to regulatory compliance and continuous improvement.
Articulates strategic commitment to understanding customer needs and exceeding expectations as organizational driver.
Expresses leadership commitment to creating enabling work environment for achieving organizational objectives.
Recognizes people engagement as essential organizational value and strategic enabler for business benefit.
Declares process approach as organizational efficiency principle for achieving desired results.
Establishes continual improvement across quality management as core annual organizational objective.
Commits organization to evidence-based decision making principle for quality management governance.
Recognizes mutually beneficial external provider relationships as strategic driver for value creation.
Declares policy commitment to social, environmental, charitable, and legislative responsibilities as organizational obligations.
Provides formal policy authorization by organizational director establishing governance authority and accountability.
Establishes the conceptual foundation for a quality policy aligned with organizational purpose and context.
Defines the policy framework as encompassing objective-setting, customer requirements, regulatory compliance, and continual improvement commitments.
Articulates customer focus as a business concept requiring understanding of needs, meeting requirements, and exceeding expectations.
Defines leadership as the conceptual responsibility for creating environments that enable objective achievement through people involvement.
Identifies people engagement as a core business concept recognizing human capabilities as essential organizational assets.
Establishes process approach as a conceptual model where activities and resources are managed as interconnected processes for efficiency.
Defines continual improvement as a central business objective across all quality management system aspects.
Establishes evidence-based decision making as a business rule requiring data analysis before quality management system decisions.
Defines relationship management as a conceptual model of interdependent external provider relationships that mutually create value.
Extends policy scope to encompass social, environmental, charitable, and legislative responsibility requirements.
Process approach principle articulating logical management philosophy that activities and resources achieve results through process or interconnected process series.
Evidence-based decision making principle establishing logical requirement that QMS decisions follow data and information analysis.
Director signature block identifying specific organizational authority and temporal validity of policy document.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Signal elements present in the ledger.
